history.diagnosis: The reward function fails to deliver the terminal success bonus to the agent and uses overly negative per-step penalties, causing the agent to exploit early termination instead of attempting to land.

The reward function penalizes vertical velocity (abs(vy)), which prevents the agent from descending, and fails to deliver the terminal success bonus, causing the agent to exploit early termination instead of attempting to land.

The dense reward lacks a strong per-step penalty for horizontal distance to pad, and the leg contact bonus rewards ground contact anywhere, so the agent learns to descend vertically and land off-pad without any incentive to move laterally toward the target.

The dense reward still lacks sufficient per-step incentive to reduce horizontal distance to the pad, while the unconditional leg contact bonus rewards off-pad ground contacts, causing the agent to land far from the target with high vertical speed and no successful touchdowns.

The agent hovers at ~11.5 units above the pad with near-zero vertical speed, never landing, because the constant alive bonus (+0.12 per step) and weak horizontal penalty (-0.05*dist) allow it to collect positive reward indefinitely without completing the task.

The agent hovers at ~11.5 units above the pad far from center, never landing, because the constant alive bonus per step (+0.12) makes staying aloft equally rewarding as descending, and the large crash penalty (−10) makes any attempt to land extremely risky, while the weak horizontal penalty (−0.05×d

The horizontal distance penalty (w_x=0.1) is too weak relative to the alive bonus (+0.42 when low), so the agent lands off-pad (dist=10.26) because staying far from the pad costs only -1.03/step but descending to ground ends the episode and resets the penalty, while centering would require costly la

The horizontal distance penalty (w_x=0.1) is too weak relative to the alive bonus (+0.42 when low), and the alive bonus is unconditional, so the agent can collect positive net reward while landing off-pad (dist=10.26) instead of moving toward the center.
The per-step reward is dominated by a constant +20 landing bonus (zero variance) that the agent collects every step without moving toward the pad, destroying any incentive for horizontal progress.

The per-step reward is dominated by a constant +20 landing bonus (mean=20.0, std=0.0, 84.6% of total) that is applied unconditionally each step, making dense shaping irrelevant and eliminating any incentive for horizontal progress.
